story_arc: A quiet first-person commute unfolds as a small emotional journey: from the muted view of the city outside, into the rhythm of the station, through the waiting and movement of the platform, and down into the underground flow where the ordinary begins to feel intimate and lived-in.
visual_rules: Use transit architecture and repeated lines as continuity anchors
Favor grounded movement over slideshow-style image swapping
Keep pacing gentle, reflective, and urban-naturalistic
Preserve cool underground tones with slight warmth in human moments
Transitions should feel like walking, riding, or looking, not presenting
narration_text:
I moved through the city quietly today,
watching it soften through glass, then fold into tunnels, lights, and passing faces.
Nothing dramatic happened,
but somewhere between the platform and the escalator, I felt held by the rhythm of it all,
as if even an ordinary commute could remember me.

scenes.title: City Through Glass
scenes.purpose: Establish the day’s reflective tone and place the viewer in a first-person journey before entering the transit system.
continuity_anchors: slightly dirty window as framing device
orange construction tarps on the right building
scenes.action_flow: The edit begins with a lingering first-person look through the window at the subdued parking lot and overcast buildings. Small implied motion in reflections or a gentle camera drift suggests being inside a moving or paused vehicle, letting the outside world feel distant and soft before the descent underground.
scenes.transition_out: Use the window frame and muted exterior lines to dissolve into the linear geometry of the station platform, as if stepping from outside observation into the commute itself.

scenes.title: Waiting at Université Concordia
scenes.purpose: Ground the story in a specific transit location and introduce the shared routine of commuters waiting together.
scenes.action_flow: The viewer stands on the platform, facing the stopped train. Attention drifts across passengers on their phones, figures inside the car, and the cool fluorescent space. The scene should feel observational and lived-in, like a held breath before movement resumes.
scenes.transition_out: Follow the line of the train and platform edge into a smoother side-on platform view, letting the cut feel like a few quiet steps forward.

scenes.title: Along the Platform
scenes.purpose: Deepen the sense of motion and interior reflection by moving from static waiting into the flow of walking and boarding.
continuity_anchors: grey tiled floor with tactile path
scenes.action_flow: The camera glides parallel to the platform, tracking the repeating doors, lights, and passing commuters. Reflections in the green wall add a doubled, inward quality, reinforcing the diary-like perspective. This scene should feel like being carried by the station’s quiet momentum.
scenes.transition_out: Let the platform’s strong lines and commuter flow resolve into the diagonal pull of the escalator, shifting from horizontal travel to vertical descent.

scenes.title: Down Into the Passage
scenes.purpose: Conclude the sequence with a sense of continuation, showing the commute as a human current rather than a destination.
continuity_anchors: colored tile pattern on side walls
scenes.action_flow: From a first-person place on or near the escalator, the viewer moves downward into the tiled underground passage. People continue ahead, storefronts and footsteps suggesting the city extending beyond the frame. The scene lands on the feeling of joining a familiar flow, not arriving at an endpoint.
scenes.transition_out: End on a natural fade or hold into ambient motion, as if the diary simply keeps going beyond this moment.
